author: Douglas Hill
message:
[docs] clarify platform resolution behavior (#22185)

This is simply based on my limited testing with fastlane 2.222.0 where I saw:

- With a Mac app binary built by fastlane existing in the file system, the deliver platform was always `osx` even when the platform was set in our config as either `ios` or `xros`.
- After deleting that binary, the platform was taken from the config.

Co-authored-by: Roger Oba <rogerluan.oba@gmail.com>

author: Roman Gardukevich
message:
[fastlane_core] Restore altool -f flag, keep -assetFile only for iTMSTransporter (#30106)

PR #30061 accidentally removed AltoolTransporterExecutor's file_upload_option
override, causing altool uploads/verifies to inherit the base class's
-assetFile behavior. altool's --upload-app and --validate-app only support
-f, so restore the override and add a verify regression test.

author: Roman Gardukevich
message:
[match] Restore renew expired certs (opt in) (#30096)

* [match] restore renew_expired_certs option and default behavior

Re-enables automatic renewal of expired certificates that was disabled by
the hotfix in #21812. Keeps the readonly guard and certificate_id changes
that landed afterwards.

* [match] restore renew_expired_certs specs

Brings back the 'renews an outdated certificate' test and the
renew_expired_certs option in related specs. Adds a regression test
ensuring an outdated certificate is not renewed in readonly mode even
when renew_expired_certs is true.

* [match] add regression tests for cases where certs must not be renewed

Even when renew_expired_certs is true, match must not delete/regenerate a
stored certificate when:
- running in readonly mode
- the cert is a developer_id type authenticated with an App Store Connect
  API token (account holder login is required for renewal via API)

* [match] clarify that renew_expired_certs does not revoke the old certificate

Document that the old certificate is only removed from match storage, not
revoked on the Apple Developer Portal, and point to `match nuke` for
freeing up certificate slots.

* [match] document single-certificate selection in renewal logic

* [match] fix rubocop line length offense in renew_expired_certs description

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Change renew certificates option to default false

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Peter Tutervai
message:
[core] Skip repeating the output again when an sh command fails (#19545)

* Skip repeating the output again when an sh command fails

If print_command_output is being set then the output was already printed while the command was running.

This only spams the logs too much, and when fastlane is used via bundler and an exception is being raised, the output is even repeated a 3rd time.

* Readded command output to the error message, but trimmed it to the last 500 characters

---------

Co-authored-by: Connor Tumbleson <connor.tumbleson@gmail.com>

author: Oliver Fox
message:
[trainer] Fix output_remove_retry_attempts not stripping failures for skipped-on-retry tests in modern xcresult parser (#30081)

PR #29887 taught output_remove_retry_attempts to drop a failure when a
test is skipped on retry, but only fixed the legacy xcresult path. In the
modern (Xcode 16+) parser, to_xml only trimmed the <testcase> XML nodes to
the final attempt while the counts (failures_count / to_hash) were still
derived from each test case's aggregate result, which xcresulttool reports
as "Failed" even when the final attempt was skipped.

Result: the JUnit showed the test as skipped (xcodebuild logged "Test
Execute Succeeded") but number_of_failures_excluding_retries stayed at 1,
so TestParser#tests_successful? returned false and scan failed CI.

Thread output_remove_retry_attempts through the modern parser's count
methods so that, only when the flag is set, counts reflect each test
case's final attempt (failed?/skipped? based on the last repetition).
Behaviour with the flag off is unchanged.

Adds a modern-path regression spec mirroring the existing legacy one.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Ricardo Sawir
message:
[scan] Handle array destination in xcodebuild_destination_parameter (#30069)

* Handle array destination in xcodebuild_destination_parameter

When scan detects devices automatically, it sets the destination
as an array of strings (e.g. ["platform=iOS Simulator,id=..."]).
However, xcodebuild_destination_parameter calls .shellescape on
the value, which fails because shellescape is a String method and
not available on Array.

This causes a warning:
  "Failed to set destination parameter for xcodebuild command:
   undefined method 'shellescape' for [...]:Array"

Fix: extract the first element when destination is an Array before
calling shellescape. For showBuildSettings and resolvePackageDependencies,
a single destination is sufficient to suppress the Xcode 13+ warning.

Closes #29873

* Fix destination array lint

* Fix destination array spec for cross-platform shellescape

The array destination tests hardcoded the Unix shellescape output
(platform\=iOS\ Simulator,id\=ABC123), which fails on Windows runners
where shellescape quotes the value instead
("platform=iOS Simulator,id=ABC123").

Derive the expected value from Shellwords.escape so it matches whatever
the current platform produces, matching the production shellescape call.

* Fix destination array spec shellescape

author: Augusto Xavier
message:
[action][swiftlint] Fix path option for SwiftLint 0.48.0 and above (#30080)

* [action][swiftlint] Fix path option for SwiftLint 0.48.0 and above

SwiftLint removed the `--path` flag in 0.48.0, so `swiftlint(path:)`
failed with `Unknown option '--path'` on newer versions. The action now
passes the path as a positional argument for SwiftLint 0.48.0 and above,
while still emitting `--path` for older versions.

* [action][swiftlint] Pass positional path after option flags for 0.48.0+

Addresses review feedback: SwiftLint usage is `lint [<options>] [<paths> ...]`, so the positional path must come after the option flags. Adds a spec asserting path ordering relative to --strict.

* [action][swiftlint] Append positional path as final argument

Addresses review feedback: append the positional path in run() after the --use-script-input-files flag so it stays the last swiftlint argument when both :path and :files are used. Adds a spec covering that ordering.

author: Marcel Hoppe
message:
[spaceship] Fix appInfoLocalizations create/delete on the Tunes API (#30084)

* [spaceship] Fix appInfoLocalizations create/delete on the Tunes API

Two related bugs prevented managing App Info localizations for a locale
that doesn't exist yet on an app:

- post_app_info_localization sent an `appStoreVersion` relationship, but
  appInfoLocalizations belong to an `appInfo`. Apple rejected the request
  ("'appStoreVersion' is not a relationship on the resource
  'appInfoLocalizations'" / "You must provide a value for the relationship
  'appInfo'"), so create_app_info_localization failed for new locales.
- delete_app_info_localization was never defined, yet
  AppInfoLocalization#delete! calls it — so every delete raised
  NoMethodError (surfaced as AppStoreLocalizationError).

Send the correct `appInfo` relationship and add the missing delete method
(mirroring delete_app_info).

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* [spaceship] Add specs for appInfoLocalizations create/delete

Cover the corrected `appInfo` relationship in post_app_info_localization
and the newly added delete_app_info_localization on the Tunes client.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

author: Augusto Xavier
message:
[precheck] Fix copyright year check to allow past years (#30076)

The copyright_date rule failed validation whenever the copyright field
did not contain the current year, rejecting otherwise valid values such
as "2016 Acme Inc.". App Store Connect only requires the copyright to be
preceded by the year the rights were obtained, which may be any past year.

The rule now passes when the copyright contains a year that is not in the
future, and still fails when the year is missing or set in the future.
